_note: Two-plane decomposition for TKT-0008 / ADR-0124 — the ui-swiper carousel family (five tags). OUTSIDE-IN nodes = the surfaces the family + its composition + its catalog touch (controls/swiper/**, the scroll track, the loop, the chrome anchors, the catalog, site, gates). INSIDE-OUT actions = what the family must DO. Crossed via hosts. plan:true — each leaf carries the checkable acceptance predicate the builder's tests satisfy; no source exists yet under controls/swiper/. Node ids align to swiper-family.spec.md §5 Trace and swiper-family.lld.md. Waves: M1 = the core (coordinator + item + track + loop + a11y + gates); M2 = the chrome anchors (pagination/paddles/label); M3 = catalog + site. The infinite loop clone-teleport (n9) + its a11y (n10) are the novelty leg; internals.ariaRoleDescription (n14/n29) is the fleet-first risk verified in the browser leg.

nodes.label: ui-swiper family (the whole intake)
the ui-swiper coordinator
tag ui-swiper, class UISwiperElement, folder controls/swiper/, extends UIContainerElement, idempotent self-define (all 5 tags on import) + idempotent #ensureParts (track + live region)
nodes.accept: `npm test swiper` — jsdom: customElements.get('ui-swiper') resolves to a UIContainerElement subclass, formAssociated false; importing swiper.ts also registers ui-swiper-item/-pagination/-paddles/-label; connect→disconnect→reconnect creates [data-part=track] and [data-part=live] exactly once (no duplicates)

nodes.label: props schema — orientation/slides-in-view/align/loop/duration/easing/pagination/paddles/active + surfaceProps, typed + reflected
nodes.accept: `npm test swiper` — jsdom: each prop's default matches SPEC-R2 ('' for the string props, false for booleans, 'horizontal'/'start' for the enums, 0 for elevation/brightness); every prop reflects to its attribute; an out-of-vocab enum coerces to the default (fail-open)

nodes.label: ui-swiper-item — UIElement, tier layout, value prop, labelAs(position) applying role=group/roledescription=slide/aria-label via its own internals
nodes.accept: `npm test swiper-item` — jsdom: customElements.get('ui-swiper-item') is a UIElement subclass; el.value defaults '' and reflects; labelAs('2 of 5') sets internals.role='group' and internals.ariaLabel='2 of 5' (read via internals); no self-driven ARIA on connect

nodes.label: the single owned scroll track — grid-auto-flow scroll container, scroll-snap, native gestures, orientation axis swap
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser swiper` — the track is the only scrolling element; horizontal wheel/drag pans it; a vertical wheel over it does NOT advance slides (no axis translation, SPEC-R3/F8); orientation=vertical swaps the scroll axis + snap axis

nodes.label: bindable active + select commit — #resolveIndex (''→first, value match, numeric index, fallback first); programmatic write no-echo, user gesture commits
nodes.accept: `npm test swiper` (jsdom: setting active resolves to the right real index via #resolveIndex — all ui-tabs cases — reflects the attribute, and emits NO event [the no-echo half, jsdom-observable]; a spied goTo is invoked with that index) + `npm run test:browser swiper` (the scroll-to-slide + a simulated user commit emits exactly one select {value,index} — the scroll half jsdom cannot observe)

nodes.label: reduced motion — goto instant under prefers-reduced-motion; teleport always instant; nothing hidden
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser swiper` (reduced-motion emulated) — the track's computed scroll-behavior is `auto` and a spied goTo takes its instant branch (no rAF animation); all slides remain present in the DOM; the loop still wraps (timing-only change)

nodes.label: infinite loop — clone-teleport: k=ceil(slidesInView)+1 clones each side, id-stripped, aria-hidden+inert+uncounted; seamless scrollLeft teleport on clone-band settle (novelty leg)
nodes.accept: `npm test swiper` (jsdom: clone count = 2k, every clone aria-hidden+inert with no id on it or its descendants, get slides excludes clones) + `npm run test:browser swiper` (INSTRUMENT-BRIDGE: scroll into the leading clone band, wait for snap settle → the track's scroll offset equals the equivalent real slide's snap target within ε and the visible content is the real slide's; exactly one select per user wrap; paddles never disable in loop mode)

nodes.label: loop a11y — position is real-index/real-count (no 'last'); [data-part=live] polite region announces on settle; clones uncounted in dots + tab order
nodes.accept: `npm test swiper` (jsdom: each real item's aria-label counts only real slides via labelAs — 'n of realCount' — read off internals; the clone→real-twin index mapping is a pure k-offset function proven directly; dots/tab-order exclude clones) + `npm run test:browser swiper` (after a real scroll settle the [data-part=live] text is 'Slide {realIndex+1} of {realCount}' — the settle jsdom cannot drive)

nodes.label: keyboard — track tabindex=0 role=group; Arrow along the orientation axis → next/prev (preventDefault); Home/End → first/last real slide
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser swiper` — real ArrowRight/Left (horizontal) move one slide and preventDefault; ArrowDown/Up do so under orientation=vertical; Home/End land on the first/last real slide; the track is a single tab stop that AT labels

nodes.label: focus safety — tab order follows real DOM order, clones inert; operated affordance keeps focus; focusing slide content never teleports
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser swiper` — keyboard-only Tab traverses real slides' focusable content and NEVER lands in a clone (inert); after a paddle advance the paddle keeps focus; focusing an input inside a real slide causes no spurious teleport

nodes.label: events — select {value,index} ⊂ allowlist, settle-only, no live mid-scroll event, no new name, not change
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser swiper` — a scroll that settles on a new slide fires exactly one select; a scroll returning to the start slide fires none; the teleport's own scroll events fire no select (the #teleporting guard); no 'change' or non-allowlist event is ever emitted

nodes.label: region ARIA — host internals.role=region + ariaRoleDescription='carousel' (fleet-first) + aria-labelledby→label or ariaLabel='Carousel'; all via internals, never host attrs
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser swiper` — the accessibility tree shows the host as a region with role-description 'carousel'; a present ui-swiper-label supplies the accessible name via element-reflection; jsdom probe reads role/label off internals and guards ariaRoleDescription where the engine lacks the setter (states?. precedent)

nodes.label: swiper.css — @scope track/slide/chrome geometry, scroll-snap, --ui-swiper-* tokens (ROLE-level colors), gap off space ladder, duration/easing overrides, vertical axis
nodes.accept: `npm test swiper` (jsdom css probe: grid-auto-columns formula present, scroll-snap-type + scroll-snap-align + scroll-snap-stop:always declared, no tokens.css edit — only --md-sys-color-* roles consumed) + `npm run test:browser swiper` (built-output: [slides-in-view=''] responsive @container column count changes across widths; [align=center|end] repoints the snap)

nodes.label: whole-shape + non-color signifier — bare swiper paints a non-collapsed operable viewport with zero CSS; the active pagination dot is size-larger, not hue-only (ADR-0057)
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser swiper` — a bare <ui-swiper> with items in a realistic container has a non-degenerate bounding box (track full width, content-driven height — the ui-slider DOT-bug guard); the active dot's computed size exceeds the idle dots' (a signifier survives greyscale)

nodes.label: ui-swiper-pagination — dots/fraction anchor the coordinator renderInto(count,active,onSelect); present anchor driven in place, boolean stamps if absent
nodes.accept: `npm test swiper` + `npm run test:browser swiper` — an author-placed <ui-swiper-pagination> renders realCount operable dots at its position; clicking a dot goes to that slide; type=fraction shows 'n / realCount'; [pagination] with no anchor stamps a default-placed dots row; a present anchor wins over the boolean

nodes.label: ui-swiper-paddles — prev/next anchor the coordinator fills with two composed ui-buttons (icon-only ghost, nav-icon sized to context), axis-rotated; boolean stamps if absent
nodes.accept: `npm test swiper` + `npm run test:browser swiper` — an author-placed <ui-swiper-paddles> holds two real <ui-button>s wired to prev/next, labelled Previous/Next slide; under orientation=vertical the icons rotate; in non-loop mode they disable at the ends, in loop mode never; [paddles] with no anchor stamps a default overlay pair

nodes.label: ui-swiper-label — author text as the accessible name; coordinator points region aria-labelledby at it (element-reflection); absent → ariaLabel='Carousel'
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser swiper` — a present <ui-swiper-label>Gallery</ui-swiper-label> makes the region's accessible name 'Gallery' via ariaLabelledByElements; removing it falls back to 'Carousel'; the label carries no role of its own

nodes.label: catalog — Swiper + SwiperItem rows (value:{prop:'active',event:'select'}, ADR-0019) + three EXCLUSION_ALLOWLIST entries (SwiperPagination/Paddles/Label), allowlist residue zero
nodes.accept: `npm test` (a2ui catalog/default/index.test.ts) — Swiper + SwiperItem validate 0-CATALOG-error with the two-way active seam; the three chrome tags resolve via the allowlist (residue-guard proves they are not also catalog keys); the whole-fleet coverage gate is green

nodes.label: descriptors — five {name}.md (swiper tier pattern / item tier layout / pagination+paddles pattern / label display) + the contract↔props/source trip-wires
nodes.accept: `npm test` — validateComponentDescriptor + compareDescriptorToProps + compareDescriptorToSource pass with zero drift for all five; swiper attributes[] mirror static props 1:1; parts (track/live), slots, events (select), customStates (ready), forcedColors declared

nodes.label: site pages (tier pattern requires {doc, demo})
swiper-doc.ts — the descriptor-derived API doc page
nodes.justify: a verification/surface node (a derived doc page), not a capability host — hosts no INSIDE-OUT action
nodes.accept: `npm test` (site-coverage.test.ts) — swiper-doc exists, derived from swiper.md (attributes/parts/slots/events/geometry), and the required-page-set check for a tier:pattern control passes

nodes.label: swiper-demo.ts — a composition demo (image gallery loop + a card deck with slides-in-view=auto, author-placed pagination+paddles+label)
nodes.justify: a verification/surface node (a demo page), not a capability host
nodes.accept: `npm test` (site-coverage.test.ts) — swiper-demo exists, satisfying the pattern tier's {doc,demo} required set, showing loop + responsive-auto + author-placed chrome

nodes.label: gates
jsdom probes — resolveIndex, active effect, clone-count/inert/id-strip math, keyboard routing, descriptor trip-wires, ARIA-via-internals reads
nodes.justify: a gate node (the jsdom probe suite) — verifies the capability hosts, hosts none itself
nodes.accept: `npm test swiper swiper-item` green, including a negative control that a programmatic active write emits no select (the binding-hygiene guard bites, not just the happy path)

nodes.label: cross-engine browser probes — whole-shape + real scroll-snap geometry + the seamless-teleport scroll-position proof + one-select-per-wrap + ariaRoleDescription + forced-colors (Chromium+WebKit)
nodes.justify: a gate node (the browser probe suite) — the INSTRUMENT-BRIDGE truth for scroll/snap the jsdom leg cannot give
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser swiper` — the seam teleport is proven by scroll-position assertion (not pixel-diff); the ariaRoleDescription role-description is read from the AX tree; forced-colors keeps the paddles + active dot visible

nodes.label: barrels/exports/size + the ariaRoleDescription fleet-first browser verification
nodes.justify: a gate node (packaging + the fleet-first-API verification obligation), hosts no capability
nodes.accept: `npm run check && npm test` green (barrels.test.ts, exports drift, tree-shake); `npm run size` run by hand — the five-tag family re-bases the family total, the marginal measured + pinned (ADR-0040); internals.ariaRoleDescription is confirmed effective in the browser leg or explicitly guarded in jsdom

nodes.label: independent component-reviewer GO before each wave commit
nodes.justify: the standing per-control-wave DoD gate (generator≠critic) — a review node, hosts no capability
nodes.accept: a fresh-context component-reviewer pass returns GO against the descriptors + anatomy + geometry + contract fidelity + the whole-shape law + the loop seamlessness, for each of M1/M2/M3

actions.label: identify itself under the fleet ui-* tag law as a non-form container coordinator, building its parts exactly once and registering its whole family on import
expose a typed, reflected attribute surface for orientation, visible-slide count, alignment, loop, duration, easing, and the chrome booleans
own exactly one scroll region positioned by native scroll-snap, letting native touch/trackpad gestures pan it and refusing to hijack page wheel
wrap arbitrary author content as an addressable, identity-bearing slide
hold a bindable active-slide identity that an agent sets silently and a user gesture commits
loop infinitely with a pixel-seamless seam the user can cross indefinitely in either direction
announce position as real-index of real-count when there is no last slide, through a polite live region
let a keyboard user move between slides along the orientation axis and to the ends
keep focus on real content across the clones and the wrap seam, never trapping or teleporting focus
emit exactly one allowlist selection event on a user-driven active-slide change, never on its own write or its own teleport
name itself to assistive tech as a carousel region with an author-supplied or default accessible name
paint a visible, non-collapsed, operable viewport with zero consumer CSS, and never signify the active indicator by hue alone
present pagination the author positions and the coordinator fills with a live dot-per-slide or fraction readout
present prev/next paddles the author positions and the coordinator fills with composed buttons, rotated to the orientation
take an author-written label element as the carousel's accessible name
become agent-emittable carousel content while keeping the placement chrome out of the catalog
tell the truth about its five elements' attributes, parts, slots, and states in per-element descriptors
respect reduced-motion by making motion instant while hiding nothing
lay out the track, slides, gap, and chrome geometry in CSS off role-level tokens, with a vertical-axis variant

edges.why: props install on the finalized class before behavior reads them
the coordinator captures ui-swiper-item children, so the item class must exist first
the track's scroll-snap behavior is realized by swiper.css
clone-teleport operates on the scroll track
position announcement counts real vs clone slides the loop creates
goTo scrolls the track into the align position
the select event is emitted by the commit path
a dot click calls goTo through the active-commit path
a paddle calls next/prev through the active-commit path
the region label is sourced from the label anchor when present
the catalog coverage gate reads the shipped descriptors
the doc page derives from the swiper descriptor
the seamless-teleport proof is the browser leg's core assertion
